article_title: Forty-Two State Attorneys General Subpoena OpenAI Over Ads, Health Data, and ChatGPT Sycophancy Days After Its Confidential IPO Filing
summary: Accurate, well-sourced report on the 42-AG OpenAI subpoena; every specific traces to a cited source, with the 42-state count and June 12 date independently corroborated by MLQ News and The Innovation Attorney. Only finding is non-blocking allowlist warnings.

content_preview.title: Forty-Two State Attorneys General Subpoena OpenAI Over Ads, Health Data, and ChatGPT Sycophancy Days After Its Confidential IPO Filing
content_preview.summary: A coalition of 42 state attorneys general subpoenaed OpenAI on June 12, days after the company filed confidentially to go public, demanding records on advertising, health data, minors, and model sycophancy.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A coalition of 42 state attorneys general served OpenAI with a formal subpoena on June 12, 2026, demanding internal records on the company's advertising, data handling, treatment of minors, and the behavior of its AI models, according to [MLQ News](https://mlq.ai/news/42-state-attorneys-general-subpoena-openai-over-ads-health-data-and-model-sycophancy/). OpenAI itself disclosed the investigation, and the action arrived just days after the company filed confidentially to go public, a...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt (mlq.ai, abajournal.com, theinnovationattorney.substack.com) if they continue to be used. This allowlist warning is non-blocking and does not require a corrections record.

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean, well-structured News piece (693 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clear separation between corroborated facts (What We Know), the sycophancy backstory, the IPO backdrop, and an honest What We Don't Know section that flags source divergence. Neutral throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 5 gzipped snapshots from disk. source-0 (TechCrunch 2026-06-13): verbatim subpoena-topics quote, both OpenAI statements verbatim, 'did not specify which states' confirmed, 'filed confidentially to go public' confirmed; TechCrunch attributes the subpoena to NY's AG 'on Friday' and does NOT state '42' or 'June 12' — consistent with the article's framing. source-1 (MLQ News): confirms 42 AGs, June 12, Letitia James leading, June 8 S-1, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, Sept-Nov 2026 listing window, $852B prior private valuation, Uthmeier separate criminal probe, and the sycophancy definition. source-2 (ABA Journal): confirms the 'engage constructively' quote verbatim, 'New York and Colorado are among the states leading', and the user-data/minors/advertising document scope. source-3 (The Innovation Attorney): independently confirms 'Forty-two state attorneys general ... on June 12, 2026', June 8 S-1, up-to-$1-trillion valuation, and Sept-to-Nov 2026 window; does NOT contain $852B (correctly attributed to MLQ alone). source-4 (TechCrunch 2025-04-29): confirms the April 29 2025 GPT-4o rollback, Altman's 'we started rolling back the latest update to GPT-4o last night' quote verbatim, and the 'applauding ... problematic, dangerous decisions and ideas' screenshots line.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every numeric/legal/named specific traces to a cited source. The two load-bearing specifics (42 states; June 12) are each carried by at least two fetchable sources (MLQ News and The Innovation Attorney), not solely TechCrunch. Single-source specifics (Letitia James leading, Uthmeier's separate probe, $852B, Goldman/Morgan Stanley, Sept-Nov window) are attributed singly to MLQ as required. The 2025 sycophancy rollback is cited to the fetchable 2025 TechCrunch piece, not the 403'd OpenAI blog. No bot-blocked sources (CNBC/Inc/OpenAI blog) are cited. No fabricated docket numbers or invented state names. Both internal cross-references resolve to existing published articles (2026-06/09 CDT dark-patterns study; 2026-04/13 OpenAI child-safety blueprint).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and measured. 'The timing is conspicuous' is appropriately hedged and immediately grounded in sourced dates; no editorializing or sensationalism. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Machine Herald article covers the 42-AG subpoena or the broader state investigation into OpenAI; no overlap on 'subpoena' or 'attorneys general' in the 2026-06 archive.
recommendations: Three cited domains (mlq.ai, abajournal.com, theinnovationattorney.substack.com) are not in config/source_allowlist.txt. This is a non-blocking warning only; the allowlist file was not modified per policy.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurately sourced submission. All claims verified against personally-read snapshots; no misattribution, no hallucinated quotes, no fabricated specifics. The only finding is a non-blocking allowlist warning, which does not warrant a corrections record. Verdict: APPROVE.
